title: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility
display_title: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility
wikitext:
[]: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by DiracSea902 » Mon May 31, 2021 4:46 pm Does the Black Moon have enough gravitational potential, along with the regular moon, to make the seas and tides rougher? If so, would it have an impact on global climate or the planet's axial tilt?

[]: Re: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon May 31, 2021 5:46 pm You make an extremely good point; my gut reaction would be to say yes, although its effect in making life on Earth living hell might be smaller compared to Second Impact and the nukes dropped in its aftermath

[]: Re: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by Archer » Mon May 31, 2021 9:13 pm Am I missing something? I thought both the white moon and the black moon are currently embedded in the earth’s crust (one in Antarctica, one in Japan - I forget which is which) and not actually in orbit around the earth where they would affect the tides.

[]: Re: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by DiracSea902 » Mon May 31, 2021 11:46 pm Archer wrote: Am I missing something? I thought both the white moon and the black moon are currently embedded in the earth’s crust (one in Antarctica, one in Japan - I forget which is which) and not actually in orbit around the earth where they would affect the tides.

[]: Re: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by Berserker » Tue Jun 01, 2021 10:54 pm The way I see it, yes, after black moon's ascend, earth's hydrosphere should've gone nuts. But the fact that black moon was mostly hollow and most probably composed of materials unknown to us makes it somewhat credible that the gravitational pull was negligible to naked eyes compared to everything that was going on all around.

[]: Re: The Black Moon and its Impact on Ocean Volatility             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Jun 03, 2021 2:06 am DiracSea902 wrote: IIRC, after Third Impact in EoE, the Black Moon that was buried beneath Tokyo-3 is currently positioned at or near Low Earth Orbit.
